old_claim.note: Cloud Computing & Distributed Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Continuous Integration depends on automated build and test execution so every integration can be verified quickly after changes reach the mainline.

source_shape.source_locator: Fowler defines CI integrations as verified by an automated build including tests and has a dedicated Automate the Build practice.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies automated build and test execution as the verification mechanism for CI integrations.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports replacing the cloud edge because CI can run on local or central build infrastructure; the required mechanism is repeatable build/test automation, not cloud service delivery.

ontology_before: Continuous Integration depended on Cloud Computing & Distributed Systems, conflating later hosted CI services and scaling infrastructure with the core build-and-test practice.
ontology_after: Continuous Integration depends on Build Automation Tools for repeatable build and test verification, while cloud remains optional hosting or scaling context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: continuous_integration now depends on build_automation_tools and no longer directly depends on cloud_computing_distributed_systems.
would_reject_if: The Continuous Integration node were narrowed to cloud-hosted CI/CD platforms only.
A stronger source showed early CI required cloud computing as a service model.
The build_automation_tools node were narrowed away from automated software builds and tests.
short_reason: CI requires build/test automation, not cloud infrastructure.
